airy circle of the First Baptist

SUFFRAGISTS TO MEET.
All persons interested in the
movement OF woman's suffrage are
requested to meet with Mrs. M.
j. Reynolds Monday afternoon at
p. IN. at the home of Mrs. Phoebe
Cox, 223 South Lincoln street.
& & &


PIANO RECITAL.


Miss Louise Fisher will present
a number of her best pupils in a
recital to be given Tuesday even
ing at the Vincent Methodist
church.


& Z Z


CLUB TO HAVE PICNIC.


The West Grove Priscilla club
will enjoy picnic at Natatorium
park June %.


MARRIED At SEATTLE.
The wedding of Miss Betty Will
liams and Edward Breeze Zane of
Spokane was solemnized Thursday
evening at the home of the brides
parents at Seattle.


Mrs. Paul Mandell Henry, sister
of the bride, was matron of honor
Jack Dorm of Spokane attended
the groom. Little Jane Stimson
was flower girl.


Mr.. and Mrs. Zane will make
their home in this city. Mr.. Zane
is manager of the Mute G Keena
Automobile company of Spokane.


i & &


DINNER AT THE CHURCH.


The members of the mems club
Of the Plymouth Congregational
church entertained the ladies last
evening at dinner served at the
church. Following the dinner a
splendid program was rendered.


Z & &
CARD PARTY.


The Yeoman will give a card
party Monday evening at their hall.
722 First avenue. All whist play.
ers are invited.


& 4 &


RECITAL ON MONDAY.
Frederick Feilberg will present
his pupils in a recital to be given
Monday evening, June 20, at the
Central Christian church.
= & 4


DR.. HERITAGE APPOINTED TO
DIRECT CHOIR.
At a meeting OF the music COM
mittee of the First Methodist
church Tuesday evening DR.. r. A.
Heritage was appointed as sue
cessor to Mr.. Brueschweiller as di-
rector of the choir.
DR.. Heritage is now at the Cen
trail Christian church and it is not
decided whether or not he will
leave.


4 = 2


3. Y. p. U. ELECTS OFFICERS.
The following officers were elect
ed for the ensuing year at meet
ing of the b. Y. p. U. held yes
terday in the First Baptist church:
President, c. II. Hart, Hillyard
Baptist church: secretary, Lillian
Pierce, Union Park Baptist church;
treasurer. Lillian Wallace, Union
Park Baptist church.; vice presi-
dents. William Barnett, Spokane
association; L. J. Neidert. Palouse
association; Grace Paris, Big Bend
association; Ralph Clark. Camas
Prairie association; Lottie Long,
Mount Pleasant association; the
selection of vice president for
the Columbia association was left
to the executive committee.


Fred Morrison. acting pastor of
the Gordon Park Baptist church
and Lillian Wallace of the Union
Park Baptist church were chosen
to represent the Eastern Washing
ton and Northern Idaho unions at
the national convention of the b.
Y. p. U. to be held July 6 to 8 in
[ cluSiyp at Sarafnpa NJ V
